question:
Will MrBeast's giveaway tweet be reposted between 3.5-3.8m times?

Description: Bulletin Board Update (6:25 PM ET): The number that shows next to the repost icon on desktop when viewing the tweet will be used for this market’s resolution. 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if MrBeast's $250k giveaway post/tweet (https://x.com/MrBeast/status/1749500209061663043) displays between 3.5 million (inclusive) and 3.8 million (inclusive) reposts/tweets 72 hours after the time it was posted (January 25, 2024, 1:32 PM ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the repost count exceeds this bracket at any point, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be this MrBeast post: https://x.com/MrBeast/status/1749500209061663043.

If the specified post is permanently deleted or the MrBeast account is banned at the time of this market's expiry date, this market will resolve to the bracket for the number reposts at the time it became unavailable.

Because posts only display accuracy up to one decimal point when in the millions, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Resolution source: https://x.com/MrBeast/status/1749500209061663043

evidences.title: MrBeast does it again and gives away some of his money on Twitter - Meristation
evidences.content: ### A new contest from MrBeast Through his Twitter / X account, the famous YouTuber announced that he will give away $250,000 to 10 random users ($25,000 if you can’t do the math). To participate, users must repost and follow MrBeast on Twitter. The YouTuber will announce the winners in 72 hours, which is next Wednesday, January 25, at 1:30 PM ET / 11:30 AM PT. > I’m gonna give 10 random people that repost this and follow me $25,000 for fun (the $250,000 my X video made) > > I’ll pick the winners in 72 hours > > — MrBeast (@MrBeast) January 22, 2024 [...] The popular YouTuber is back with his challenges and we have 72 hours to be one of the lucky ones to get a piece of his fortune thanks to Twitter/X. Gabriel Huerta Update: Share facebook twitter whatsapp Make us preferred on Google The famous YouTuber MrBeast is still at it, and whether you love him or hate him, you might be interested to know that you can make some money, well, a lot of money, thanks to his latest giveaway on social media. ### A new contest from MrBeast [...] This is not the first time that MrBeast has done this kind of contest where he gives away money to his followers. Just last May, Jimmy Donaldson gave awa...

evidences.title: MrBeast Statistics on X: "It's been 24 hours since MrBeast posted his first long-form video on X! Let's see how it did... 👀 👁️ VIEWS and IMPRESSIONS: The YouTube version of this video had ~49.9M views at the 24-hour mark of its upload. The X version of this video has ~72.5M impressions at its 24-hour https://t.co/J2vJmz9Urx" / X
evidences.content: It's been 24 hours since MrBeast posted his first long-form video on X! Let's see how it did... 👀 👁️ VIEWS and IMPRESSIONS: The YouTube version of this video had ~49.9M views at the 24-hour mark of its upload. The X version of this video has ~72.5M impressions at its 24-hour mark! It is important to note that YouTube views do NOT effectively compare to X impressions due to differing metric-tracking methods. It is common for X impressions to be inflated, as they do not require users to view the video in full. Unfortunately, we do not have the metrics to accurately compare these two posts at this time. 👍 LIKES: The YouTube version of this video had ~3.08M likes at the 24-hour mark of its upload. The X version of this video has ~287K likes at its 24-hour mark! 🗨️ COMMENTS: The YouTube [...] ~287K likes at its 24-hour mark! 🗨️ COMMENTS: The YouTube version of this video had ~83.1K comments at the 24-hour mark of its upload. The X version of this video has ~12K replies at its 24-hour mark! 📈 Some other exciting statistics on the new X post... - The post has ~28K retweets at its 24-hour mark! - It has been reposted by multiple notable accounts, including @elonmusk, @X, and @XBusiness....

evidences.title: MrBeast reveals he made $250,000 from X video - BBC News
evidences.content: "Advertisers saw the attention it was getting and bought ads on my video (I think) and thus my revenue per view is [probably] higher than what you'd experience," he wrote in the post, external, adding that he planned to choose 10 "random people" to give the money he made. Analysts said an equivalent haul would be hard to repeat without MrBeast's massive profile. "He said he made $250,000, so not bad for one video," said Karsten Weide, principal at W Media Research. "It's good numbers but you have to have a massive amount of traffic." The amount so-called influencers can make varies from person to person. The terms of individual deals are kept confidential, although it is thought the biggest names online may be able to negotiate special rates. [...] # MrBeast reveals he made $250,000 from X video Mr Beast waving at the camera The world's most popular YouTuber, MrBeast, has revealed he made more than $250,000 (£197,000) from posting a video on X, formerly known as Twitter. He had previously said it was not worth posting on the social media site as creators only got a small amount of advertising revenue. However, after a U-turn last week, he posted an old video - which has generate...

evidences.title: MrBeast made more than $250K from posting a video on X fka Twitter. Sounds promising, right? But there's 3 considerations here: 1. Elon may have been pumping views on the video - Mashable reports… | Eric Wei | 10 comments
evidences.content: from Mashable: Chris Jackson, graphic On opportunity cost to Mr Beast, isn’t his opportunity cost Zero? If he just took earning videos from his YouTube and posted on X, who really cares if it made only 1/10th or 1/100th the Adsense revenue so it costs nothing to Mr Beast? We have found that Twitter is a uniquely different audience than other social medias, so he wouldn’t be cannibalizing his content for X at the expense of lost views on YouTube (in fact, all social media platform audiences appear to be different to us. Even between Instagram v. Facebook, the Facebook audience will skew much older less savvy vs IG. Reddit audiences are the smartest/highest IQ & most loyal if not tricked/gamed, Twitter are the dumbest and most toxic & skews more foreign-based…and so on) [...] No alternative text description for this image Eric Wei, graphic from Mashable: Chris Jackson, graphic [...] # Eric Wei’s Post View profile for Eric Wei MrBeast made more than $250K from posting a video on X fka Twitter. evidences.title: MrBeast
evidences.content: Getty Images MrBeast began his YouTube channel in 2012. Twelve years later, it had attracted more subscribers than any other account on the platform. The teenager initially posted gaming videos of himself playing _Minecraft_, _Call of Duty_, and other titles, as well as clips with commentary about other YouTubers. He uploaded his first viral stunt in January 2017, a nearly 24-hour video of himself counting to 100,000 (in reality, it took him about 40 hours). Soon after the video, the 19-year-old reached more than 1 million subscribers. [...] Finding a formula for virality, MrBeast has continued to make videos featuring unusual stunts or large acts of philanthropy. These have included giving $10,000 to a homeless person as well as tipping restaurant workers with gold bars and luxury items. In September 2020, MrBeast launched a spinoff channel called Beast Philanthropy, which claims all of its ad revenue, merchandise, and sponsorship earnings go to charitable causes. ## Other YouTube Stars Image 25: PewDiePie in December 2015PewDiePie Image 26: Jake Paul in February 2023Jake Paul Image 27: Colleen Ballinger Miranda Sings PhotoColleen Ballinger Advertisement - Continue Reading Belo...

evidences.title: X appears to be juicing MrBeast's views as Elon Musk tries to woo ...
evidences.content: The creator appeared shocked") that his post had over 100 million views. (As of publication, Mr Beast's post has 128 million views.) However, on X, views aren't counted like they are on video platforms like YouTube. On the contrary, those "views" are actually just impressions on the post itself, not the number of people who played MrBeast's video. In May 2023, Musk had X remove the public video view count from the platform, so it's unclear now how many actual views a video gets on X. In his latest post, MrBeast has asked") his followers to vote on just how much they think X will pay him as part of the ad revenue share program: $10, $10,000, $100,000, or $1 million. [...] MrBeast has promised to share just how much he makes from X's monetization program from this test. So, we'll soon find out the answer to that question. Whatever MrBeast makes though, it seems clear that X put its fingers heavily on the scale to make sure circumstances favored a massive view count. Topics Social Media X/Twitter Elon Musk Creators [...] It's almost impossible to tell how much MrBeast will rake in due to how X's creator monetization program works. Jimmy Donaldson, aka MrBeast.Courtesy Steven Kahn via MrBeast Add NBC News to Google By Daysia Tolentino Jimmy Donaldson, the YouTuber known as MrBeast, is used to evaluating the value of different products in his videos. Now, he's putting another comparison to the test: X vs. YouTube. [...] Donaldson uploaded his first video directly to X on Monday, a reupload of his YouTube video "$1 vs $100,000,000 Car!" which he initially posted in September. The YouTube upload of this video has received over 211 million views. "I’m curious how much ad revenue a video on X would make so I’m reuploading this to test it. Will share ad rev next week," the creator said in an X post. The video had over 52 million views on X as of Tuesday morning. evidences.title: MrBeast Scams: Verified Accounts, DeepFakes Used in ... - Tenable
evidences.content: The comment provides a link to the website The website uses a cartoon likeness of MrBeast and says that over $450,000 has already been given away and that a $500 reward has been reserved for the current page visitor. In order to receive the alleged $500, users are instructed to click on the “CLAIM REWARD” button and complete a survey. [...] MrBeast, the most popular YouTube creator as of October 2023, has been impersonated in a variety of scams on YouTube and TikTok, including a recent deepfake promoting a fake free iPhone 15 giveaway ## Background [...] In addition to completing these offers, users are asked to share personally identifiable information, including their names, addresses, phone numbers, and more. This information is sold to third parties for marketing purposes. ## Free iPhones aren’t free Even in the case of the supposed free iPhone 15 giveaway, the website users are directed to asks for credit card information in order to “pay for delivery.” If MrBeast can afford to purchase and giveaway 10,000 iPhones, surely he could afford to pay for the delivery costs. Users that are directed to these websites may not realize what they are signing up for when providing their...

evidences.title: Marketing Case Study | MrBeast
evidences.content: This is the phase when MrBeast cracked the algorithm. From counting to 100,000 to exploding dry ice and building absurd cup forts, Jimmy Donaldson reverse-engineered viral content through persistence, experimentation, and an intuitive grasp of behavioral psychology in marketing. Then came the breakthroughs: -Counting to 100,000 -Fidget Spinner VS Face -I Bought One Snickers Bar From Every Walmart It looked like absurd performance art. But it was also research. He was reverse-engineering virality. That understanding turned into a machine: production teams, analytics labs, storytelling pipelines. And what started as one kid sitting in his room has now become a scaled system with global reach: -450 million subscribers on his main channel -Nearly a billion followers [...] -Nearly a billion followers -Billions of views -A candy company -A Theme Park -An anime show -Mobile games And now Beast Games on Amazon, which has already been renewed for several more seasons. And the wildest part? He’s only 27. He’s just getting started. Press enter or click to view image in full size Image 7: Grid of MrBeast’s most viral YouTube thumbnails featuring extreme challenges, giveaways, and stunts; a...

evidences.title: Paolo Poli | LinkedIn - LinkedIn
evidences.content: He goes extreme on one thing. On LinkedIn you go deep on one topic your ICP cares about. "I audited 100+ LinkedIn profiles this quarter. 90% make the same 4 mistakes:" "I analyzed every post we wrote last month. The ones that booked calls all had this:" The key with all of these (and this is the part that separates Mr Beast from every other YouTuber)... The HERO of every Series is the product. Feastables shows up in the challenges. The brand IS the content. On LinkedIn, the hero of your Series has to be your expertise and your service. I see this across 200+ accounts: 1. Never develop a Series (random posts, no pattern, audience has zero expectation) 2. Develop a Series but the hero is their personality (go viral, book zero calls) 3. Develop a Series where the hero IS their service [...] calls) 3. Develop a Series where the hero IS their service (pipeline gets printed) Mr Beast figured this out on YouTube. David Ogilvy figured it out on TV 70 years ago (he called it the Big Idea and said it has to work for 30 years). Same game on LinkedIn. Build the Series. Make your product the hero. Run it until post 50 hits as hard as post 1. What Series would you build first? [...] - Steal M...
